Customers Overview
Live data · February 2026 (MTD)
May 1 – May 26 · 26 days
M
Customers AI · Overview
High confidence2,140 customers · 8,420 ordersSynced just now
Equity leaking
Your base is worth $12.4M — but projected to fall $520K
Customer equity rose all quarter, yet new-cohort retention is down 8pp. The leading indicators say the front door is leaking faster than acquisition fills it. Revenue still looks fine; equity doesn't.
VIP signal
8 VIPs going cold — $185K of equity exposed
Top spenders past 1.5× their personal cycle. Protecting one VIP relationship is worth roughly ten new acquisitions — start here, before the broader win-back.
Equity engine
Your subscription creates customers at 71% — and you barely sell it
It has the highest repeat-creation rate of any product but only 180 sold. Routing acquisition toward it is the cheapest way to grow equity rather than rent orders.
MTD · Net Customer Equity added
+$1.2M
Net lifetime value added this month — new and expanding customers minus churn. Positive now, but the projection turns negative as newer cohorts retain worse.

The five forces on your customer equity
Acquisition
Quality of new customers
0.95×
Paid Meta — your biggest source — never pays back. Email & Organic return 5–7×. You're partly renting orders, not building equity.
Product Affinity →
Retention
New-cohort retention
41%
Month-1 retention has slipped from 49% to 39% across cohorts. The loyal base masks it, but the front door is leaking.
Retention →
Expansion
Breadth & LTV lift
2.1×
Multi-category buyers are worth 2.1× — but 58% of the base has only bought one category. The biggest untapped lever.
Segments →
Churn
Going cold
98
98 customers slipping past 2× their cycle — $185K exposed, $95K recoverable if reached now, top 20 first.
Retention →
Satisfaction
The review tail
7%
A 4.7★ average hides it: 1–2★ customers repeat at 7%, and 60% of bad reviews are late delivery — an Ops fix.
Reviews →
Where customers come from · what brings them back
Acquisition source
per 100 new
Where this period's new customers entered.
4119159511
Organic41Paid Google19Direct15Paid Meta9Social5Other11
Paid Meta brings 9 of every 100 — and almost none of them come back. Volume here flatters acquisition while draining equity.
What brings them back
per 100 repeat orders
Which mechanism triggered each repeat purchase.
3422191285
Email flows34Discount codes22Organic return19Retargeting12SMS8Unknown5
A fifth of repeats are bought with discounts — the least valuable returns. Email-driven repeats are the ones that compound.
Ask Magellan anything…
